seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Eaglehawk Neck and East Devonport. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Eaglehawk Neck vs East Devonport: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Eaglehawk Neck and East Devonport.
heading: Eaglehawk Neck vs East Devonport — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Eaglehawk Neck, Tasmania and East Devonport, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Devonport has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Eaglehawk Neck (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Eaglehawk Neck or East Devonport safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Eaglehawk Neck has a flood risk score of 45/100, while East Devonport scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Eaglehawk Neck scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. East Devonport scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Eaglehawk Neck or East Devonport?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Devonport has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
